title: [test] pagination paging, defaults, and invalid-param handling on GET /api/notes
acceptance_criteria: In tests/notes.test.ts add tests: with >20 notes, default returns 20 items and correct total/page/limit/totalPages; page=2&limit=10 returns the right slice; pagination applies after tag/q filter; invalid page/limit (0, negative, non-integer, limit>100) return 400. npm test passes.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=M model=standard doer_tokens=1400 reviewer_tokens=616 output_usd=null

title: [impl] Update existing GET /api/notes tests for new paginated response shape
acceptance_criteria: In tests/notes.test.ts, update the four existing GET /api/notes tests (empty, returns all, tag filter, search) to unwrap res.body.data instead of res.body, and assert against the new envelope. npm test passes (no leftover assertions against res.body as a bare array for the list endpoint).
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=S model=standard doer_tokens=600 reviewer_tokens=264 output_usd=null

title: [impl] Add page/limit pagination to GET /api/notes handler
acceptance_criteria: In src/api/notes.ts GET / handler: parse page (1-based int, default 1) and limit (int, default 20, max 100) query params, applied AFTER tag and q filtering. Invalid/out-of-range page or limit returns 400 with {error:...}. Response shape becomes {data:[...],total,page,limit,totalPages}. tag and q params unchanged. npm run build passes. Maps to gh-toy-534.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=M model=premium doer_tokens=1400 reviewer_tokens=616 output_usd=null

title: [test] length validation rejects oversized title and content on POST and PUT
acceptance_criteria: In tests/validation.test.ts (and/or notes.test.ts), add tests: 201-length title and 10001-length content each return 400 with the exact field/message from the spec, on both POST and PUT; boundary values (200 title, 10000 content) are accepted. npm test passes.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=S model=standard doer_tokens=600 reviewer_tokens=264 output_usd=null

title: [impl] Enforce title (<=200) and content (<=10000) length limits in validation
acceptance_criteria: In src/utils/validation.ts, validateCreateInput and validateUpdateInput reject title>200 chars with {field:'title',message:'Title must be 200 characters or fewer'} and content>10000 chars with {field:'content',message:'Content must be 10000 characters or fewer'}. Existing valid requests unaffected. npm run build and existing tests pass. Maps to gh-toy-t5x.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=S model=standard doer_tokens=600 reviewer_tokens=264 output_usd=null

title: [test] updatedAt advances on PUT /api/notes/:id
acceptance_criteria: In tests/notes.test.ts, add a test under PUT /api/notes/:id that creates a note, captures createdAt/updatedAt, performs a PUT, and asserts response updatedAt > original updatedAt (and createdAt unchanged). Use a small delay or mock to guarantee a distinct timestamp. npm test passes.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=S model=cheap doer_tokens=600 reviewer_tokens=264 output_usd=null

title: [impl] Set updatedAt to current timestamp in noteStore.update()
acceptance_criteria: In src/models/note.ts, noteStore.update() sets updatedAt to new Date().toISOString() (no longer preserves existing.updatedAt) while still preserving id and createdAt. npm run build passes. Maps to gh-toy-btt.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=S model=cheap doer_tokens=600 reviewer_tokens=264 output_usd=null

title: sprint: noteapi p1 fixes
description: Sprint to implement top 3 P1 issues: fix updatedAt on PUT, add input length validation, add pagination to GET /api/notes

title: Fix updatedAt not being set on PUT /api/notes/:id
description: When a note is updated via PUT, the updatedAt field is not refreshed — it keeps the value from creation. This makes updatedAt misleading and useless for cache invalidation or sorting by recency. Fix noteStore.update() in src/models/note.ts to set updatedAt to the current ISO timestamp whenever an update is applied.

title: Add input length validation for title and content
description: POST and PUT requests accept title and content fields without any length constraints. Long inputs waste memory and can cause downstream issues. Add validation: title max 200 chars, content max 10000 chars. Return 400 with a clear error message when limits are exceeded. Apply this in validateCreateInput and validateUpdateInput in src/utils/validation.ts.

title: Add pagination to GET /api/notes
description: The GET /api/notes endpoint returns all notes with no limit, which will degrade performance as the store grows. Add page and limit query parameters so clients can page through results. Default limit should be reasonable (e.g. 20). Return pagination metadata (total, page, limit) alongside the data array.

title: CLI CRUD commands (list/read/create/update/delete)
description: Implement five subcommands against the NoteAPI: list (optional --tag/--q filter), read (--id required), create (--title, --content required), update (--id required, --title/--content optional), delete (--id required). Each command calls the API and prints results to stdout. Non-zero exit on API error.

title: CLI help system and input validation
description: The CLI must support --help/-h flags globally and per subcommand, printing usage and exiting 0. Input validation must reject empty or whitespace-only arguments with a clear error message and non-zero exit code. No stack traces in error output.

title: Add input validation for empty or blank strings
description: When user passes empty string or whitespace-only string as argument, reject with clear error message. Non-zero exit code. Add unit test. Ref: gh-toy-v6z.

title: Add --version flag to CLI
description: The CLI tool should support a --version (or -v) flag that prints the current version string and exits with code 0. Acceptance: running ./tool --version prints fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0, exit code 0, works alongside other flags.

title: Configure CI to run on pmlite-e2e/* sprint branches
description: GitHub Actions CI does not trigger on the pmlite-e2e/* branch pattern used by pm sprints. Update the workflow trigger rules to include this pattern so sprint branches get CI coverage before PRs are merged.

title: Add config file support (~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml)
description: Allow users to persist default flag values in a YAML config file at ~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml so they don't have to repeat common flags on every run. Acceptance: reads config on startup, CLI flags override config, warns on unknown keys, documents supported keys in README.

title: Handle SIGINT gracefully (Ctrl-C)
description: The tool should catch SIGINT (Ctrl-C) and exit cleanly without a Python traceback or incomplete output. Acceptance: Ctrl-C prints 'Interrupted.' and exits with code 130, no stack trace shown, partial output files cleaned up.

title: Add JSON output mode via --json flag
description: Add a --json flag so all command output can be emitted as machine-readable JSON for scripting and CI pipelines. Acceptance: --json flag accepted on any subcommand, output is valid JSON, human-readable output is default, errors also JSON-formatted.

title: Tag filtering endpoint
description: GET /api/notes?tag=work returns only notes with that tag. Already partially implemented - needs tests.
